none: Bowhead whales, Proposed regulations governing the taking of bowhead whales during 1980 for subsistance, environmental assessment (EA) B1; Special report to the International Whaling Commission (1978) B2; Early development and reproduction (1979) : environmental impact statement.

This document details the "Bowhead Whale Field Studies" proposed by the U.S. Department of the Interior's Alaska Planning Group (APG) in Alaska. It outlines a project to study the distribution and abundance of white whales, specifically the bowhead whale, in the eastern Beaufort Sea. The APG seeks to establish a National Wildlife Refuge to protect this vital population. The project involves a comprehensive survey using satellite imagery and traditional methods to assess the whale’s presence and estimate their numbers. The goal is to inform conservation strategies and ensure the long-term health of this unique species within the Alaskan Arctic ecosystem. The study aims to provide data for future management decisions regarding the Beaufort Sea’s wildlife.

It outlines a project to study the distribution and abundance of white whales, specifically the bowhead whale, in the eastern Beaufort Sea. The APG seeks to establish a National Wildlife Refuge to protect this vital population. B. Biolcgical Background; Jo DEStTELDUELON; Michael F. Tillman; William M.

Marquette; Eskimo Whalers; Beaufort Seas; David Strait; Tomillin; Uellen; Zimushko

The project, initiated in 1975, aimed to assess the impact of whaling on the Alaskan marine ecosystem and propose a plan to mitigate the consequences of this historical practice. The initial focus was on understanding the distribution of whales and assessing the potential ecological damage caused by whaling, leading to a comprehensive report detailing the risks and proposing a conservation strategy. However, due to ongoing concerns about the impact of whaling on the ecosystem, the project was ultimately deemed unsuccessful, with the proposed plan never being fully implemented, and the Alaskan National Wildlife Refuge ultimately being established as a permanent conservation area.
